rule.desc: Prefer immutable objects and defensive copies across threads.
rule.rationale: Immutability eliminates entire classes of race conditions.

rule.desc: Use `java.util.concurrent` utilities instead of manual synchronization.
rule.rationale: Executors, queues, and atomics are safer and more scalable.

example.code: ExecutorService pool = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(4); Future<Result> result = pool.submit(task);

rule.desc: Keep synchronized sections minimal and lock on private objects.
rule.rationale: Reduces contention and avoids accidental lock sharing.

rule.desc: Use `Atomic*` for simple counters; use `synchronized` or locks for compound invariants.
rule.rationale: Atomics are fast but only safe for simple operations.

rule.desc: Document whether a class is thread-safe or not.
rule.rationale: Clear contracts avoid unsafe sharing and subtle bugs.
